mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously as long as FEDFUNDS is declining month-over-month.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.28% as of April 2008 reflects Fed easing from prior tightening cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in < 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings expectations downward in response to weakening demand signals.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.0% in April 2008, elevated from prior year
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 50% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales decline signals weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations. When RSAFS falls month-over-month, equity investors reduce growth forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 378148.0 million dollars in April 2008
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 52% of instances when RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization and future earnings potential. Sustained month-over-month declines in INDPRO signal supply-side weakness and reduced profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as the market reprices long-term growth.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 48% of instances when INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts and mortgage demand jointly signal future consumer wealth effects and construction sector health. When HOUST falls below 950k annualized units while FEDFUNDS remains above 2%, the market prices in both demand destruction and constrained monetary accommodation, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 947.0k in March 2008, FEDFUNDS at 2.28% in April 2008
regime_hint: housing crisis with constrained monetary policy
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint condition) shows no incremental predictive power beyond individual series (Rank IC of interaction < Rank IC of HOUST alone).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment and unemployment rate jointly signal labor market momentum. When PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises simultaneously, the market interprets this as accelerating job losses and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.1pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137818.0k in April 2008, UNRATE at 5.0%
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in < 58% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power beyond UNRATE alone (Rank IC of interaction < Rank IC of UNRATE).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and monetary policy stance measured by FEDFUNDS jointly determine real interest rates and equity risk premiums. When CPI inflation rises while FEDFUNDS falls, the market faces conflicting signals: monetary accommodation versus eroding purchasing power. This regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as uncertainty increases.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month AND FEDFUNDS declines > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 213.743 in April 2008, FEDFUNDS at 2.28%
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 54% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect shows no incremental explanatory power beyond individual series (Rank IC of interaction < max(Rank IC of CPIAUCSL, Rank IC of FEDFUNDS)).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly reflects aggregate demand strength. When GDPC1 growth decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate, the market reprices equity valuations downward over a 60-day horizon as investors recognize late-cycle or recessionary conditions. This mechanism is regime-specific to periods of slowing growth.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11693.1 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2008
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 50% of instances when GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year rolling window.
